title: Automated Decision-Making and Profiling (ADMP)
description: Regulatory regime for automated decision-making and profiling under the JPDP ADMP Guideline issued 30 April 2026 (BM-only). Anchored in Section 7 (notice — mandatory FACT of ADM disclosure), Section 38 (withdrawal of consent), and Section 40 (sensitive personal data conditions). The issued guideline is materially narrower than PCP No. 3/2025 — biometric and CCTV measures are deferred to a separate forthcoming guideline; the three PCP-named ADM Restrictions (Refuse/Information/Human Review) are NOT carried as named rights. See `removedFromPCP` for the full divergence list.

guidelineStatus.issuingAuthority: Personal Data Protection Commissioner Shariffah Rashidah Syed Othman
guidelineStatus.verificationNote: Verified against issued JPDP ADMP Guideline (April 2026, BM-only, 731 lines vs PCP 1039 lines). The issued guideline is materially NARROWER than the PCP. Major divergences: (a) the three named ADM Restrictions (Right to Refuse / Information / Human Review) are NOT carried as named rights — replaced with s7 notice + s38 withdrawal anchoring; (b) Right to Human Review and 21-day SLA NOT in issued text; (c) Right to Information softened from 'shall' to 'may' (boleh) with trade-secret/IP carve-out; (d) Biometric data measures (PCP §2.26-2.32) DROPPED entirely; (e) CCTV measures (PCP §2.33-2.35) DROPPED entirely; (f) AI 'no manipulation' safeguard DROPPED; (g) 'explicit consent' (PCP §2.18) downgraded to plain 'persetujuan' for ADMP exception; (h) NEW: ADMP triggers DPIA regardless of subject count (§6.1).

legalBasis.description: Act 709 does not yet specifically address automated decision-making and profiling. The ADMP Guideline introduces the regulatory regime under the Commissioner's authority. Cross-references s4 (definition of biometric data, included in sensitive personal data per A1727), s5 (mandatory PDP Principles compliance), s6 (consent), s17 (sensitive personal data conditions), s45 (general exemptions, replicated for ADMP exemptions).

structure.definitions: automated-decisions/definitions.json — automated decision-making, profiling, biometric data; predictive vs inference distinguishing elements (§4.2)
structure.triggers: automated-decisions/triggers.json — Ambang ADMP (legal effects / significantly affects); DPO earliest engagement; DPIA regardless of subject count
structure.rights: automated-decisions/data-subject-rights.json — issuedRequirements (Section 7 notice + Section 38 withdrawal + Section 40 sensitive PD); pcpDerivedRightsForReference (Right to Refuse, Right to Information, Right to Human Review — flagged as NOT in issued)
structure.exceptions: automated-decisions/exceptions.json — §9.9 Exceptions (Kekecualian) — contract / legal compliance / consent (NOT explicit consent); §9.12 Section 45 Exemptions (cross-reference)
structure.ai: automated-decisions/ai-generative-ai.json — 7 AI best practices (§10.2.1–10.2.7), permissive verb 'boleh menerapkan'; PCP no-manipulation safeguard flagged as removed
structure.biometric: automated-decisions/biometric-data.json — PCP-derived measures retained for analyst reference; NOT in issued ADMP Guideline (sourceType: pcp-derived-pending-issuance)
structure.cctv: automated-decisions/cctv.json — PCP-derived measures retained for analyst reference; NOT in issued ADMP Guideline (sourceType: pcp-derived-pending-issuance)

coreObligations: Identify which processing operations constitute automated decision-making or profiling (Ambang ADMP — §7.1)
Assess whether each operation produces legal effects (kesan undang-undang) or significantly affects (kesan signifikan) data subjects

Where triggered: notify data subject of the FACT of ADM/profiling under s7 (mandatory). Explanation of logic, reasons, and likely impact is permissive ('boleh') and may be withheld for confidentiality, trade secret, IP, or proprietary rights
Engage DPO from earliest stage of any ADMP design (§5.1)
Conduct DPIA for any ADMP regardless of nature or extent (§6.1) — quantitative threshold is supplementary, not gating

For sensitive personal data: process only under s40 conditions (explicit consent OR s40(1)(b) grounds — employment law / vital interests / medical / legal proceedings); controllers MAY consider technical and organisational safeguards
Where consent is the lawful basis: Section 38 withdrawal of consent is the operative right (the issued guideline does not create a standalone Right to Refuse beyond s38)

AI / Generative AI used for ADMP: 7 best-practice items (Section 10.2.1–10.2.7) — risk assessment before deployment, dignity/accuracy/no-inappropriate-use, AI-disclosure in privacy notice, mitigate over-reliance, operator training, AI not the only factor, trained reviewers

removedFromPCP.note: These provisions appeared in PCP No. 3/2025 but did NOT survive into the issued guideline. They are retained in this repo as PCP-derived analyst content, NOT JPDP-issued requirements.
items: Right to Refuse as a standalone named right (s38 withdrawal of consent is the operative mechanism)
Right to Human Review (no equivalent in issued text; no 21-day SLA)
Right to Information 'retained even where Exceptions apply' (PCP §2.16) — issued guideline weakens transparency obligation to permissive 'boleh' with carve-outs
Explicit consent requirement for ADMP exception (PCP §2.18) — downgraded to plain consent
Biometric data 7 categories and 6 measures (PCP §2.26-2.32) — likely deferred to a separate biometric guideline
Children/minors-in-ADMP discussion — no equivalent in issued text

internationalAlignmentNote: The issued JPDP ADMP Guideline does NOT explicitly cite GDPR, UK GDPR, NPC Circular, PIPL, EU AI Act, or EU DSA. Cross-references below are analyst-supplied for cross-jurisdictional comparison only — they are NOT issued JPDP requirements and must NOT be cited as such in audit findings.

internationalAlignmentAnalyst.gdpr: Article 22 (right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing); Article 4(4) (definition of profiling) — analyst reference
internationalAlignmentAnalyst.uk: UK GDPR Article 22 + ICO guidance — analyst reference
internationalAlignmentAnalyst.philippines: NPC Circular on automated decision-making — analyst reference
internationalAlignmentAnalyst.indonesia: Personal Data Protection Law profiling provisions — analyst reference
internationalAlignmentAnalyst.china: Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL) automated decision-making provisions — analyst reference
internationalAlignmentAnalyst.euAiAct: Risk-based AI regulation overlapping where AI systems make decisions affecting individuals — analyst reference
internationalAlignmentAnalyst.euDsa: Digital Services Act — bans profiling-based ads for special category data; bans targeted ads to minors — analyst reference (relevant to children's-ADMP best practice since PCP §2.57 prohibition was dropped from issued JPDP guidelines)

evidenceExpectations: DPO Design-Engagement Records (§5.1 mandatory earliest involvement)
Transparency Disclosure Log (including Trade Secret/IP carve-out rationales)
AI Operator Training Registry (records of mandatory staff training)
DPIA Report for ADMP (regardless of subject count)

crossRepoLinks.AI-Governance: Cross-link AI/Gen-AI controls — ADMP triggers AI accountability obligations; ADMP §10.2 best practices align with NIST AI RMF and EU AI Act analyst references in AI-Governance repo
crossRepoLinks.dpia: ADMP §6.1 mandates DPIA for any in-scope ADMP regardless of subject count. The PCP >10,000-ADM-subjects quantitative threshold was REMOVED from the issued DPIA Guideline (Q7 qualitative factor instead). See dpia/thresholds.json for the current 2-quantitative + 7-qualitative framework.
crossRepoLinks.dpbd: DPbD's 4 issued elements (Sifat Proaktif / Perlindungan Hujung-ke-hujung / Ketelusan / Berpusatkan Pengguna) apply to ADMP design. NOTE: PCP §2.57 prohibition on profiling/marketing of children was NOT carried into either the DPbD or ADMP issued guidelines — children's-ADMP rules now rest only on DPbD §5.3 parental consent for under-18 subjects.

newRequirementsAdded.note: Issued requirements that are NEW vs PCP. Auditors should verify these specifically — they are the most likely points where existing programmes fall short.
items: §5.1 — DPO must be engaged from the earliest design stage of any ADMP (not deferred to fieldwork or pre-launch review)
§6.1 — DPIA mandatory for any in-scope ADMP regardless of nature or extent (subject count is no longer gating)
§6 flowchart — explicit assessment path including 2-yearly DPIA refresh for ADMP-related processing
§9.3 — Mandatory s7 notice that ADM/profiling is occurring (the FACT must be disclosed; logic explanation under §9.4 is permissive)
§9.4 — Permissive logic explanation with explicit carve-outs for confidential information, trade secrets, intellectual property, proprietary rights
§10.2.3 — NEW: Privacy notice must disclose AI use (where AI is involved in ADMP); not required by PCP
§4.2 — Predictive (ramalan) and inference (inferens) distinguishing elements are now formally adopted as part of the profiling test
§3.2 — Partially-automated processing is explicitly in scope (PCP framing was 'solely automated')
